stories.title: White House’s ‘lack of organization’ has AI lobbyists fretting
stories.summary: The Trump administration’s shifting stance on regulating artificial intelligence is fueling fresh uncertainty for the tech industry. Seven lobbyists and policy advisers told POLITICO they’ve struggled to get specifics on a possible executive order that could impose tough vetting rules on new AI models, a major question that has gotten mixed messages from the White House in recent days. “There is no clarity,” said one senior tech lobbyist, who like others in this piece was granted anonymity to de

stories.title: UK election mauling leaves Keir Starmer promising hope — but little change
stories.summary: LONDON — MPs from Britain’s governing Labour party despair at what they brand a lack of decisiveness from their leader. Fortunately for Keir Starmer, they can’t decide what to do about it. The U.K. prime minister appears trapped in Downing Street for now as his critics circle amid heavy midterm election losses for their party — yet have no immediate plan to boot him out. While results were still rolling in Friday, Starmer responded by vowing to stay put, fight on, and improve his message to the

stories.title: Hungary's new PM to be sworn in during 'regime change' party
stories.summary: Nearly a month ago Péter Magyar steered his Tisza party to a landslide victory, sweeping away 16 years of rule by Viktor Orbán.

stories.title: Trump’s tantrums over Nato are prompting European leaders to think the unthinkable | Paul Taylor
stories.summary: Europeans must urgently seek to close their security gaps in case Russia attacks – and the US refuses to defend its allies Donald Trump’s war in Iran and tirades against Nato allies are accelerating moves to develop a plan B for European security in case the US is no longer willing to help defend allies against a Russian attack. Europe must prepare for sudden vulnerability gaps if the fickle US president decides to pull out key military enablers before Europeans can develop their own alternative

stories.title: Hungary: New govt intent on initiating swift systemic change
stories.summary: Peter Magyar and his government are determined to push ahead with change at full throttle. But even before the cabinet is sworn in, there has been a "resignation." And questions remain about Magyar's Ukraine policy.

stories.title: Fico: Személyesen még nem ismerem Magyar Pétert, de arra kell törekednem, hogy megértsük egymást
stories.summary: A szlovák miniszterelnök ugyanolyan szintű kapcsolatot akar az új magyar kormánnyal, mint Orbán Viktor idején.

stories.title: Szimbolikus gesztusokkal kezdett Magyar Péter, az igazi kihívások azonban csak most jönnek
stories.summary: A ma megalakuló Országgyűlés feladata, hogy visszaadja a parlamenti munka értelmét, amit az elmúlt években teljesen kiüresített a Fidesz. Miközben az uniós források miatt az idő is szorítja a Tisza Pártot, gyökeres változtatásokra lesz szükség a törvényhozásban, ha valódi rendszerváltást szeretnének. Ennek biztosan része, hogy nagyobb mozgásteret kell adni az ellenzéknek és a sajtónak.

stories.title: „Börtönbe!” – A félresikerült szlovák elszámoltatási kísérlet tanulságai
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt egyik fontos ígérete, hogy feltárják az Orbán-rendszerben elkövetett bűnöket, a felelősöket pedig elszámoltatják. Szlovákiában kudarc lett a vége az ugyanerre irányuló 2020-as próbálkozásnak, Robert Fico pedig diadalmasan térhetett vissza pár évvel később.

stories.title: Három ember van a világon, aki mesterhármasig jutott BEK-döntőben, Puskás Ferenc kétszer is
stories.summary: Május 30-án a sorozat történetében először rendezheti meg Budapest a Bajnokok Ligája döntőjét, a Puskás Arénában ünnepelhet Európa legjobb futballcsapata. A fináléig mindennap visszatekintünk a döntők egy-egy nagy pillanatára, egyéniségére, érdekességére. BL-sztorik, harmadik rész.

stories.title: Huth Gergely szerint nem fér bele, hogy Orbán a Vadhajtásokat tekinti igeforrásának
stories.summary: A Pesti Srácok főszerkesztője röviden reagált a távozó miniszterelnök médiafogyasztási szokásairól szóló mondataira.

stories.title: Megérkezett a Tisza-frakció a parlamentbe, hamarosan átveszik a hatalmat – élőben a rendszerváltás napja
stories.summary: Történelmi nap: 16 év után a Tisza átveszi a kormányzást a Fidesztől, megalakul az új parlament, Magyar Pétert megválasztják miniszterelnöknek. Egész napos élő adás a rendszerváltásról.

stories.title: A Fidesz legszebb reményei váltak valóra a Nemzeti Petíció válaszaiban
stories.summary: Az Orbán-kormány utolsó hivatali napján tette közzé a milliárdokból finanszírozott felmérés eredményeit, illetve kiderült a költségek egy része is.

stories.title: Üdvrivalgás fogadta a Kossuth téren Magyar Pétert, hamarosan megalakul az új parlament
stories.summary: 10 órakor kezdődik a parlament alakuló ülése. Magyar Pétert 14:30-kor, nyílt szavazással választják miniszterelnöknek, de előtte még esküt tesznek a képviselők és titkos szavazással Házelnököt is választanak. 16 órától a Kossuth téren ünnepel a Tisza, a protokoláris program és Magyar Péter beszéde után éjszakába nyúló bulira készülnek.

stories.title: VSquare: Russian spy working under diplomatic cover quietly expelled from Hungary after election
stories.summary: According to the paper’s sources, Hungarian counterintelligence had recommended Sushkov’s expulsion as early as February, but the Orbán government blocked the move.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor megvan
stories.summary: „Hát megvan?”, kérdezik a leköszönő miniszterelnöktől saját munkatársai a Facebook-oldalára feltöltött videóban.

stories.title: Új fesztivállal ünneplik Kossuth-díjas költőjüket a pécsiek
stories.summary: A könyvbemutatók és felolvasások mellett tartanak dalszöveg-irodalomról szóló beszélgetést, színházi előadást és zenei programot is.

stories.title: Százmilliók tévés nagypapája, aki a bolygó élő lelkiismeretévé vált
stories.summary: Százéves lett David Attenborough, aki szenvedélyes kíváncsiságával és stílusával a természetfilmezés megkerülhetetlen alakja. Túl nagy fogai miatt először még képernyőre sem kerülhetett, később a BBC egyik irányítója lett. Neki köszönhetjük a Monty Pythont és a snookerközvetítéseket is.

stories.title: Az eltűnt 1,7 milliárd forint elsodorta a Győr-Szol elnök-vezérigazgatóját, lemondott posztjáról
stories.summary: Sokkal jobb éves eredményt produkált, mint az elődje, a lakáskassza-ügy azonban úgy tűnik, elsodorta a győri mamutcég első emberét. Az igazgatóság elfogadta a döntését.

stories.title: Magyar Péter helyett öccse lett a vezérigazgató a tanácsadó cégében
stories.summary: A kormányfői poszt miatt kellett lemondania a vezérigazgatóságról, de továbbra is ő a tulajdonosa LLM Zrt.-nek.

stories.title: Krasznahorkai László közel tíz év után újra színpadra lép Magyarországon
stories.summary: A Nobel-díjas író legutóbb 2017 őszén vállalt nyilvános fellépést idehaza.

stories.title: A Filmintézet működésének folytonosságát és az SZFE autonómiájának helyreállítását kérik rövid távon a magyar filmesek
stories.summary: Bemutatta a javaslatcsomagját a magyar filmeseket tömörítő Filmreform. A szándékok egybevágnak azzal, amit a Tisza politikusa ígért az Eurocine megnyitóján.

stories.title: Nem tudtak egyedi érintettséget megállapítani a magyar kormány uniós kémügyében „a hírszerzőkén túl”
stories.summary: A magyar állami hírügynökség tudósításából történetesen lemaradt a hírszerzők érintettségéről szóló félmondat.

stories.title: Ha Magyarország csatlakozik az Európai Ügyészséghez, az visszamenőlegesen is vizsgálhat magyar ügyeket
stories.summary: Laura Codruța Kövesi európai főügyész Magyarországon múlik, mikortól kezdődhet a szervezet hatásköre, ami nem kormányokat, hanem bűncselekményeket vizsgál.

stories.title: A magyarok háromnegyede szerint Magyarország profitált az EU-s tagságból
stories.summary: Az Ukrajnának nyújtott uniós támogatásokkal viszont nagyon nem értenek egyet.

stories.title: Három héttel a bukás után leért a legaljára Orbán
stories.summary: Tíz cím a távozó miniszterelnök által naponta olvasott, és legfontosabb igeforrásának nevezett Vadhajtásokról, mindenki okulására.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor nyíltan kimondta, hogy az Index egy fideszes újság
stories.summary: A bukása után, az utolsó miniszterelnöki munkanapján felvállalta, ami eddig is nyilvánvaló volt.

stories.title: Luxusüzletben szelfizett Mészáros Lőrinc a francia riviérán
stories.summary: A leggazdagabb magyar az egyik legértékesebb luxusmárka, a Hermès üzletében járt, pár nappal korábban pedig az általa gyakran használt privát jet épp egy közeli repülőtéren landolt.

stories.title: Kiderült, mennyiért dolgozott Szijjártó Péter kommunikációs stábjában Gáspár Evelin
stories.summary: Gáspár Győző lányának szerződése májusig szólt volna, de a tárca idő előtt felbontotta.

stories.title: Háromnapos tűzszünetet jelentett be Donald Trump Ukrajna és Oroszország között
stories.summary: Trump szerint a hétfőig tartó tűzszünet alatt teljesen felfüggesztik a katonai műveleteket, emellett a két ország ezer-ezer hadifoglyot is kicserél.

stories.title: Vilseck, Germany: A town on edge over US troop withdrawal
stories.summary: If US President Donald Trump is serious, 5,000 US soldiers could be withdrawn from Germany. This would include the Stryker Brigade in Vilseck. The town is in shock.

stories.title: Péter Magyar's first cabinet crisis: the brother-in-law who had to go
stories.summary: Melléthei-Barna is a lawyer and a university peer of Hungary's next prime minister. He was one of the founding 10 members of the Tisza Party when it was formed in 2020 and served as the party's legal director.

stories.title: Hungary's new government pushes for euro by 2030
stories.summary: Hungary's new leadership wants the country to adopt the euro by the end of the decade while repairing strained ties with Brussels. But with a weak economy and tight deadlines, experts warn the path will be steep.

stories.title: Romania's government falls, far-right firewall crumbles
stories.summary: Romania's Social Democrats have joined forces with the far right to oust the pro-reform government of Ilie Bolojan. The economic impact will be serious; the political cost high. Is the far right on the brink of power?

stories.title: Romania's government collapses after Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan loses confidence vote
stories.summary: Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan has been voted out of office by lawmakers. Romania's coalition was driven apart by unpopular austerity measures, with the Social Democrats leaving and moving to oust the PM.

stories.title: Nigel Farage will need to be 22 times more efficient to win the next general election
stories.summary: LONDON — Judged on his own record, Nigel Farage is one of the most prolific political failures in modern British history.  In three of the five elections he has fought over the past 20 years under various party banners, none of his side’s candidates won a single seat — and he finally entered Westminster in 2024 only after failing in seven previous attempts. But local elections across England, as well as parliamentary votes in Wales and Scotland, this week confirmed that Farage’s Reform UK p

stories.title: Trump, with no evidence, claims Congo sent prisoners to US border with Mexico
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump on Friday claimed that the Central African country of Congo released inmates from its prisons and sent them to the U.S. border with Mexico. The president offered no proof or evidence to back up his claim. “They emptied the prisons of the Congo into the area of the southern border, and they told them to just walk in, because stupid Americans are going to accept you beautifully,” Trump said at a Friday Mothers’ Day event at the White House. “And these were hard, mean, v

stories.title: What Beijing has learned about the US from the Iran war
stories.summary: The increasingly intractable conflict between the U.S. and Iran is revealing American military and strategic vulnerabilities — and offering important lessons to its biggest rival. China is watching as the U.S. fails to cut through an Iranian blockade and expends heavy firepower, the Trump administration struggles to extricate itself from an unpopular war, global gas prices soar and the Pentagon’s strategic documents reveal that warding off Beijing is  no longer the top priority . As U.S. Pr

stories.title: Four south Florida men convicted in Haitian president’s assassination
stories.summary: Men were convicted in Miami federal court for plotting to kill Jovenel Moïse at his Port-au-Prince home in 2021 Four south Florida men were convicted on Friday of plotting to kill the Haitian president, Jovenel Moïse , in 2021 by hiring mercenaries to assassinate him at his Port-au-Prince home, court records show. Prosecutors argued during the nine-week trial in a Miami federal court that the men assembled two dozen former Colombian soldiers and supplied them with money, guns, ammunition and tac

stories.title: Virginia Democrats file motion asking for stay in map ruling as Trump touts ‘huge win’ for Republicans – as it happened
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. Virginia supreme court strikes down new congressional maps in win for Republicans Sign up for the Breaking News US email A reminder that my colleagues are covering the latest on the conflict in the Middle East. Including secretary of state Marco Rubio’s visit to Rome, to mend strained relations with Italian leaders and the Vatican after Donald Trump chided Pope Leo XIV for his stance on the war in Iran. Rubio told reporters in Rome that the US should get a response

stories.title: FCC’s warnings on political interviews ‘chill’ First Amendment, ABC says
stories.summary: Disney’s ABC lambasted the Federal Communications Commission’s newfound  efforts to regulate interviews on broadcast talk shows , warning that Chair Brendan Carr’s “unprecedented” actions threaten to “chill” the First Amendment and stifle coverage of political candidates. The regulatory filing marked the latest stage in a confrontation between the broadcasting networks and Carr, who has used his tenure atop the nominally independent FCC to wage a series of fights against perceived foes of U

stories.title: Trump says there will be 3-day ceasefire in Russia-Ukraine war
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said Friday that Russia and Ukraine have agreed to a three-day ceasefire and prisoner exchange mediated by the United States, the latest twist in the yearslong conflict that has seen other pauses quickly fall apart. The temporary ceasefire will stretch from Saturday to Monday to facilitate the transfers of 2,000 Russian and Ukrainian prisoners of war, Trump said on social media. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy quickly signaled his support, thanking Trump for helpin

stories.title: David Pocock applauds $387m in extra funding for CSIRO after tens of thousands sign petition
stories.summary: Analysis commissioned by independent senator found national science agency’s funding is at its lowest since 1978 Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Albanese government will boost funding to CSIRO by $387.4m in a bid to meet the long-term costs of the national science agency. It follows months of advocacy by scientists and staff after hundreds of job cuts and cost-cutting measures. Continue reading...

stories.title: France has a record number of presidential hopefuls. Will any of them be able to hold back the far right?
stories.summary: About 30 people – nearly all men – have expressed an interest in taking on the far-right National Rally in next year’s ballot At a Paris meeting hall this week, hundreds of leftwing voters braved a rainstorm to gather chanting: “Unity! Unity!” They were celebrating the 90th anniversary of France’s Popular Front, a leftwing alliance that was formed in the 1930s amid fears that the far right could take power. But their concerns were more immediate. Continue reading...

stories.title: Worried Britons ‘prepping’ for major disruption with stash of tins and cash, survey shows
stories.summary: Fears over a natural disaster or cyber attack are pushing households into contingency planning, Link survey shows Millions of Britons are “prepping” for a potential “major disruptive event” by keeping a stash of cash at home, stockpiling tinned goods or ensuring they have a battery-powered torch close to hand, new data suggests. With war raging in the Middle East and Ukraine , extreme weather becoming more frequent, and warnings that the UK’s critical infrastructure is at risk from cyber-attacks

stories.title: ‘We are talking about energy security for Europe’: Norway doubles down on oil and gas production
stories.summary: Norway’s energy minister says country has a ‘responsibility’ to address shortfalls caused by wars in Ukraine and Middle East In case of any doubt about Norway’s commitment to maintain – and expand – its production of gas and oil offshore, the energy minister, Terje Aasland, has a pithy response: “We will develop, not dismantle, activity on our continental shelf.” This week, to the alarm of environmental campaigners, he announced that three gasfields off the country’s southern coast would reopen

stories.title: ‘They have screwed each other pretty badly’: tensions emerge in Netanyahu-Trump alliance
stories.summary: Israeli PM says he has ‘full coordination’ with US president amid reports that Washington no longer consults him Benjamin Netanyahu interrupted an uncharacteristically long silence over the Iran conflict this week with a video commentary insisting he had “full coordination” with Donald Trump, with whom he spoke “almost daily”. The insistence that all was rosy in the US-Israeli relationship followed weeks of reports in the domestic press that Israel was no longer being consulted over the Iran con

stories.title: Mysterious disappearance of man on popular WA hiking track sparks police search
stories.summary: Samuel Whitsed, 27, is believed to have travelled from Victoria to hike Bibbulmun Track near outskirts of Perth Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Three months after a hiker was last seen at a popular walking track, police have become increasingly concerned for the missing man’s welfare. Police have scoured dense bushland for a Victorian man after he disappeared from a popular hiking trail on the other side of the country. Continue reading...

stories.title: Germany's sugar tax sparks 'nanny state' debate
stories.summary: Friedrich Merz's government has agreed to introduce a sugar levy on soft drinks as part of its health care reforms. Critics have called it disproportionate interference, but dozens of countries already have such a levy.

stories.title: Exclusive: Iranians risk arrest at Iraq border to escape Tehran's total internet blackout
stories.summary: Desperate to communicate with the outside world, Iranians are increasingly travelling to the border with Iraq, where Iraqi SIM cards and impromptu hotspots became a hot commodity, they told Euronews.

stories.title: Why a weakened Starmer will worry the EU
stories.summary: After bruising local election losses, Sir Keir Starmer is preparing a renewed push for closer ties with the European Union — but Brussels worries that his political weakness could stall or derail any meaningful post-Brexit reset.

stories.title: UK elections 2026: 5 takeaways from Labour’s drubbing
stories.summary: LONDON — Britain’s electoral map is being redrawn once again by Nigel Farage. His Reform UK insurgency is fracturing politics — and Keir Starmer is emerging the biggest loser of it all. But even as the right prospers, left-wing populists in the Green Party are also making big gains off the back of the prime minister’s unpopularity. All in all, a chaotic picture is being painted as votes are counted in elections for a swathe of English councils as well as devolved parliaments in Scotland and Wale

stories.title: Trump administration relaxing hunting restrictions in US parks and refuges
stories.summary: After January order from US department of the interior, managers across 55 sites have lifted prohibitions Donald Trump’s administration is quietly pushing national park, refuge and wilderness area managers to dramatically scale back hunting restrictions, raising questions about visitor safety and the impact on wildlife. Doug Burgum, the US Department of the Interior secretary, issued an order in January directing multiple agencies to remove what he termed “unnecessary regulatory or administrativ

stories.title: General Motors to pay $12.75m settlement for selling drivers’ location and data
stories.summary: Automaker had given ‘numerous statements reassuring drivers that it would not do so’, says California attorney general General Motors (GM) agreed to pay $12.75m to resolve claims that it illegally sold hundreds of thousands of Californians’ location and driving data to two data brokers, said the state’s attorney general, Rob Bonta, on Friday. He said this came after the Detroit-based automaker had given “numerous statements reassuring drivers that it would not do so”. “General Motors sold the da

stories.title: Russia and Ukraine confirm three-day ceasefire from 9 May
stories.summary: Both sides also confirm prisoner swap which Donald Trump announced along with suspension of all ‘kinetic activity’ between the warring states Donald Trump has announced a three-day ceasefire in the war between Russia and Ukraine from 9 to 11 May. The US president said on social media the ceasefire would include a suspension of all “kinetic activity” and a swap of 1,000 prisoners from each country. It was confirmed by both sides. Continue reading...

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Zelenskyy ‘hereby decrees’ Moscow can hold Victory Day parade
stories.summary: Ukraine president says ‘Red Square shall be excluded’ from any attacks, while Kyiv, Moscow and Trump proclaim three-day ceasefire. What we know on day 1,536 Continue reading...

stories.title: AI will make language barriers disappear – and diminish our understanding of other cultures
stories.summary: Machines may soon translate every conversation flawlessly. But language is more than information – it is curiosity, intimacy and cultural discovery One of my earliest assignments as a young interpreter was to provide simultaneous interpretation for the proceedings of an ecumenical council that brought together all Christian denominations. As my homework, I dutifully read scripture, the gospels, papal encyclicals and the conclusion of the first council of Nicaea. There was, however, one thing I h

stories.title: Evacuation of hantavirus-stricken cruise ship could face delays due to bad weather
stories.summary: ‘Unprecedented operation’ under way to receive MV Hondius off Tenerife to assess and repatriate those onboard • What is hantavirus? • Where did the cruise ship hantavirus come from and what happens next? The evacuation of the hantavirus-stricken MV Hondius cruise ship must be completed within 24 hours of the vessel reaching Tenerife on Sunday or face days or even weeks of delay because of bad weather, authorities in the Canary Islands warned on Friday. The Dutch-flagged vessel, which was sailing

stories.title: 2026 elections mapped: how Labour lost ground in different directions
stories.summary: Keir Starmer’s party lost out to Reform and the Greens, with no respite in Scotland, Wales or England. These maps show the scale of the historic results Full results from England, Scotland and Wales Labour has suffered heavy losses across England, Scotland and Wales, losing ground to opponents on the left and the right in a fragmented political system. The graphics below show where Labour’s losses were most severe, and how the electoral landscape has changed as a result. Continue reading...

stories.title: 2026 election results: latest from local, Scottish and Welsh votes
stories.summary: From devolved parliaments in Scotland and Wales to councils and mayoralties in England, find out what happened in your area Elections mapped: how Labour lost ground in different directions Continue reading...

stories.title: The SNP may have won again but Scottish politics has been upended
stories.summary: Public frustration gave the SNP a muted victory, while Labour tied with Reform for second and the Greens claimed a ‘seismic’ result Long before the final votes were counted in Scotland, veteran Labour politicians said it was a defeat made in Downing Street. When the Scottish Labour leader, Anas Sarwar, strode into the Glasgow count arena on Friday afternoon flanked by sombre-faced activists, the scene was a mirror image to the same venue in 2024, when his resurgent party won 36 seats from the Sc

stories.title: Barrister says ‘dead woman was put on trial’ after husband cleared of manslaughter
stories.summary: Charlotte Proudman’s comments follow trial of Christopher Trybus, who was acquitted of all charges against wife Tarryn Baird A barrister has suggested that a “dead woman was put on trial” in the case of Christopher Trybus, who was cleared of manslaughter by a jury. Charlotte Proudman’s comments came after Trybus was found not guilty by a jury of eight women and four men, who deliberated for more than 40 hours. He was acquitted of all charges: manslaughter, coercive and controlling behaviour and

stories.title: Hong Kong dissident Nathan Law on China spies in UK: ‘We’re not surprised’
stories.summary: Law says conviction of two men for spying raises serious concerns about how they accessed sensitive information Nathan Law, an exiled leader of the Hong Kong student protest who lives with a £100,000 bounty on his head from the Chinese authorities, was not surprised to discover a spy ring had photographed him entering the Oxford Union for an evening debate in November 2023. The conviction at the Old Bailey of Chi Leung “Peter” Wai, 38, and Chung Biu “Bill” Yuen, 65, for assisting a foreign intel

stories.title: Frustrated by Iran, Trump at last seizes enriched uranium – but from Venezuela
stories.summary: US energy department says 13.5kg of uranium taken from reactor in Caracas – a fraction of the 408kg held by Tehran Donald Trump has succeeded in removing a country’s stash of highly enriched uranium – although that country is not Iran . On Friday, the US Department of Energy announced that “thanks to President Trump’s decisive leadership” 13.5kg (about 30 pounds) of uranium had been removed from a legacy research reactor in Venezuela . Continue reading...

stories.title: Americans on hantavirus cruise ship reportedly to be quarantined in US
stories.summary: US CDC personnel are reportedly meeting ship in Canary Islands to accompany Americans on a flight to Nebraska The US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) is reportedly sending personnel to the Canary Islands to meet the cruise ship affected by the hantavirus outbreak , with plans to accompany American passengers back to the US on a chartered flight and place them into quarantine in Nebraska. An additional CDC team is already headed to Nebraska, according to unnamed sources who spoke

stories.title: Iran war: US expecting Tehran's response to proposed deal
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said Washington was expecting an answer from Tehran over a proposed deal to set up talks to end the war by Friday night Eastern time. Follow live.

stories.title: Mexico to shorten school year due to World Cup, summer heat
stories.summary: Mexican authorities announced a decision to end the academic year earlier than planned because of the World Cup and a heat wave. But the decision sparked backlash from parents' groups and teachers.

stories.title: Journalist found dead in Colombia's conflict zone
stories.summary: Mateo Perez, a 25-year-old Colombian reporter, reportedly went missing on May 5 after being detained by guerrilla members at a roadblock.

stories.title: Eurovision 2026: Politics collide with a blockbuster show
stories.summary: Controversy over Israel's participation and boycotts unprecedented in scale are already weighing on the Eurovision Song Contest before it even kicks off in Vienna.

stories.title: Worldwide race to trace passengers from hantavirus-hit cruise ship
stories.summary: About 12 countries have so far been linked to the hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius, as the UN health agency confirms at least five cases.

stories.title: Hovering objects and flashing lights: what we learned from UFO documents released by the Pentagon
stories.summary: The US has published transcripts, video clips and audio recordings about unidentified flying objects, also called unidentified anomalous phenomena.

stories.title: Australian by-election a litmus test for right-wing One Nation Party
stories.summary: Victory for the populist party in Farrer, New South Wales, would see One Nation's first MP elected to Australia's national parliament.

stories.title: Move over matcha. Hojicha is coming to a cafe near you
stories.summary: From lattes to ice-cream, the Japanese tea is popping up more and more on menus across the UK.

stories.title: Anti-work anthems, Boy George and controversial lyrics: A guide to all 35 Eurovision songs
stories.summary: From dancefloor bangers to heartfelt ballads, here are the highlights and low points of Eurovision.

stories.title: From trusted aide to biggest rival: Suvendu Adhikari set to become West Bengal chief minister
stories.summary: A former ally of Mamata Banerjee, the BJP leader will be West Bengal’s next chief minister.

stories.title: Anger and resignation in Tenerife as hantavirus ship approaches
stories.summary: Some on the Spanish island worry that the imminent arrival of the MV Hondius could pose a health risk.

stories.title: International cyber attack disrupts swathe of universities and schools
stories.summary: A hacking group breached the academic software Canvas, used by thousands of schools and universities across the globe.

stories.title: Cooking kosher in Damascus, a complicated matter for Syria's Jewish community
stories.summary: Syria's small Jewish community has struggled to maintain kosher traditions after it dwindled in size, relying on imported meat and individual effort to keep centuries-old practices alive in Damascus.

stories.title: Trump declassifies 162 UFO files for public to judge the 'evidence'
stories.summary: A first tranche of files dating back to 1948 has been released to provide greater transparency regarding unidentified flying objects. Although the archive features unusual sightings reported by astronauts and pilots, it contains no definitive proof of alien life.

stories.title: Iran accuses US of 'reckless military adventure'
stories.summary: Abbas Araghchi says the US attacks each time there is a diplomatic solution on the table.

stories.title: Oil prices rise after US and Iran exchange fire in Hormuz strait
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump says the ceasefire between Washington and Tehran is still in place.

stories.title: Who is coming to Putin’s Victory Day parade — and who is not?
stories.summary: For the Russian president, Victory Day is arguably the most important public holiday of the year. But this time, even Russia’s former allies are staying away, leaving the Kremlin alone in its much scaled-down celebration.

stories.title: Iraq announces huge oil find near Saudi border as Hormuz crisis bites
stories.summary: Iraq has the fifth largest proven oil reserves in the world, estimated at 145 billion barrels, representing about 17% of total Middle Eastern reserves and around 9% of global reserves, according to the US Energy Information Administration (EIA).

stories.title: South Africa president faces call to resign after court ruling
stories.summary: The Constitutional Court says MPs were was wrong to block impeachment proceedings against Cyril Ramaphosa.

stories.title: Greece examines mystery naval drone found in Ionian Sea
stories.summary: Greek media say the drone, which reportedly had explosives, could be either Ukrainian or linked to the Russia-Ukraine war.

stories.title: Woman jailed in Somalia for peaceful protest ‘stripped, kicked and beaten’
stories.summary: In an exclusive interview from prison, Sadia Moalim Ali, a 27-year-old rickshaw driver, tells of her treatment after being arrested for demonstrating against the government A woman being held in prison in Somalia for taking part in peaceful protests has described how she was tortured by her guards. Sadia Moalim Ali, 27, told the Guardian she was stripped naked by two male guards in a room monitored by CCTV, kicked, beaten with a baton and left for two days in a small cell without food. Continue

stories.title: Tensions high in West Bengal after BJP aide shot dead and hundreds arrested
stories.summary: Turmoil and violence rocks state after prime minister Narendra Modi’s party claimed victory in legislative election Tensions have been high in the Indian state of West Bengal after a top political aide from Narendra Modi’s party was shot dead in the street and hundreds were arrested as violence broke out following elections this week. The prime minister’s Bharatiya Janata party (BJP) claimed victory in the West Bengal elections on Monday , defeating Trinamool Congress (TMC), which had ruled over

stories.title: Trump ally Roger Stone condemned for providing lobbying services to Myanmar’s military junta
stories.summary: Stone being paid $50,000 a month to ‘rebuild’ relations between Washington and Myanmar’s military-backed government The US lobbyist Roger Stone, a longtime friend and ally of Donald Trump, has been condemned for accepting $50,000 a month to “rebuild” relations between Washington and Myanmar’s military-backed government. Myanmar’s leaders have been internationally isolated since seizing power in a coup in 2021 , and have repeatedly been accused of atrocities that may amount to war crimes . Activi

stories.title: UAE says air defences engaging with Iranian attacks – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed US-Iran ceasefire under threat after exchange of strikes in strait of Hormuz The European Union is set to tell airlines the impact from the Iran war on tourism is not yet severe enough to justify emergency measures for the sector, draft EU guidelines seen by Reuters showed. “The current situation does not point to the need for dedicated measures for the tourism sector, unlike during the COVID-19 crisis,” said the draft EU guidelines, which the European Commission is due t

stories.title: Australian woman accused of joining Islamic State in Syria refused bail
stories.summary: Janai Safar, 32, will remain in custody in Sydney. Two other women facing separate charges are expected to apply for bail in Melbourne on Monday Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast An Australian woman charged with joining Islamic State in Syria has been refused bail despite concerns expressed by her lawyer over the loss of connection it would cause with one of her family members. Janai Safar, 32, appeared before New

stories.title: UK to summon Chinese ambassador after convictions over spying on Hong Kong dissidents
stories.summary: The two men were found to have carried out intel gathering missions on political dissidents residing in the UK wanted by Chinese authorities. One witness reported the pair threatened to arrest him on foreign soil due to his continued opposition.

stories.title: UAE reports drone and missile attack from Iran as ceasefire is challenged
stories.summary: Meanwhile, a shipping data company reported that Iran had created a government agency to vet and tax vessels seeking passage through the crucial Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Three hikers dead after Indonesia’s Mount Dukono volcano erupts
stories.summary: Two Singaporeans and a local person were in no-go zone when they were killed, say officials Three hikers – two Singaporeans and an Indonesian – have died in an eruption of Indonesia’s Mount Dukono volcano, where they found themselves in a no-go zone, officials said. The eruption, on Halmahera island, sent an ash cloud about 6 miles (10km) into the air, with no towns or villages near enough to face any immediate threat. Continue reading...

stories.title: Future of UK lies in the EU after Brexit ‘failure’, say European Greens
stories.summary: Exclusive: Co-chair Vula Tsetsi says it is time to trigger debate, as statement is agreed at annual leadership meeting in Brussels The European Green party has urged the UK to consider rejoining the EU and draw a line under the “political and economic failure” of Brexit. A text declaring that “the United Kingdom’s future lies in the European Union” was adopted by a large majority of European green parties at the movement’s annual leadership meeting on Friday, the day before Europe Day. Continue

stories.title: Iran war costs Toyota £3bn as prices of materials soar and sales fall
stories.summary: Carmaker gives one of biggest warnings yet of conflict’s impact on businesses while Trump tariffs also take toll Toyota has reported a £3bn hit from costs from the war in Iran, as prices of parts and materials soared and sales dropped. The world’s biggest carmaker said profits declined in its financial year to March as it was “likely unable to absorb newly added impact from the Middle East”, in one of the largest warnings yet of the war’s impact on businesses. Continue reading...

stories.title: Canadian high school where deadly mass shooting occurred to be torn down
stories.summary: Tumbler Ridge secondary school was site of February mass shooting in which nine were killed and dozens injured The school that was the site of one of Canada’s deadliest mass shootings will be torn down, officials have announced. The decision to demolish the Tumbler Ridge secondary school came after meetings between the school board and survivors, family and community members, said the British Columbia premier, David Eby. Continue reading...

stories.title: US awaiting response from Iran over proposals for ceasefire deal, says Rubio
stories.summary: Diplomatic efforts continuing despite fighting in and around contested strait of Hormuz in recent days Middle East crisis – live updates The US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, has said that Washington is expecting a response from Iran on Friday to its proposals for an interim deal to end the conflict in the Middle East, as Iran accused the US of breaching the increasingly fragile ceasefire announced last month. In recent days there have been the biggest flare-ups in fighting in and around the c

stories.title: Vatican stresses need for peace as Rubio meets pope amid strained relations
stories.summary: US secretary of state speaks with Leo and officials after tensions following attacks by Trump over Iran war The Vatican has said it raised the “need to work tirelessly in favour of peace” in talks with the US secretary of state, Marco Rubio, who is in Rome on a trip widely regarded as an effort to ease tensions after Donald Trump’s repeated criticisms of Pope Leo. Amid unprecedented strain on relations between the Holy See and Washington, Rubio was received by the pope on Thursday at the Apostol

stories.title: The week around the world in 20 pictures
stories.summary: Femen and Pussy Riot protest in Venice, Israeli strikes in Gaza, the hantavirus outbreak and Emma Chamberlain at the Met Gala – the past seven days as captured by the world’s leading photojournalists Continue reading...

stories.title: Germany news: Exports jump higher than expected
stories.summary: Exports from Germany had risen at a time when many feared they would be in retreat because of punishing US tariffs. Meanwhile, one of Germany's biggest banks is set to cut jobs to fend off a takeover.

stories.title: Germany: AfD benefits from discontent with Merz's government
stories.summary: Never before has a German government been this unpopular after only one year in office as that of Chancellor Friedrich Merz. The far-right AfD is capitalizing on this.

stories.title: Why Russia has scaled back May 9 Victory Parade
stories.summary: This year's May 9 military parades in Moscow will be much more modest and have been canceled altogether in other parts of Russia. The Kremlin has blamed Ukraine.

stories.title: Pioneer in the fight against AIDS: Botswana's ex-President Festus Mogae died
stories.summary: The former President of the Republic of Botswana, Festus Mogae, has died. The democrat was considered the architect of the Southern African country's economic rise and a pioneer in the fight against AIDS.

stories.title: Fertilizer shortages: What are Africa's options during the Hormuz crisis?
stories.summary: The ongoing blockade of the Strait of Hormuz is affecting African nations particularly badly. But there are potential solutions for mitigating the drastic effects on the continent in the short, medium and long term.

stories.title: UAE charts its own course across the Middle East and Africa
stories.summary: Close relations with Israel and the United States have placed the United Arab Emirates in Iran's crosshairs as Abu Dhabi pursues a more independent foreign policy than many Gulf countries, including Saudi Arabia.

stories.title: New film portrays Thomas and Erika Mann — with an oddly twisted premise
stories.summary: Premiering at Cannes and starring Sandra Hüller, Pawel Pawlikowski's "Fatherland" is set during the Cold War — a complex period for the prominent anti-Nazi intellectuals.

stories.title: Christians decry persecution in India's heartland
stories.summary: With Christians in India increasingly targeted by Hindu vigilantes, victims say police often side with the attackers.

stories.title: Hantavirus: Spain readies to receive cruise ship
stories.summary: Spanish authorities are readying to receive the cruise ship at the center of the hantavirus outbreak. The WHO has confirmed six hantavirus cases linked to the ship so far.

stories.title: How real is a coup threat against Russia's president?
stories.summary: Increased security around Vladimir Putin and a scaling back of public appearances have given rise to suspicions of a coup or assassination attempt. Yet some observers remain skeptical.

stories.title: US: Pentagon releases trove of classified UFO files
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump hailed his government's move to release over a 100 UFO-related documents as "complete and maximum transparency."

stories.title: Trump announces 3-day ceasefire between Ukraine and Russia
stories.summary: The US president said both sides had agreed to halt fighting from May 9 to 11 and to exchange 1,000 prisoners each. Both Ukraine and Russia confirmed the ceasefire and prisoner exchange. Follow DW for more.

stories.title: Pakistan: Drop in Gulf remittances puts families on edge
stories.summary: For millions of households, money sent by Pakistani workers in the Gulf offers a lifeline, helping their families cover basic needs such as food and education.

stories.title: Canvas education platform back online after cyberattack
stories.summary: The hacker group ShinyHunters had sought to extort universities before the cyberattack on the Canvas education platform took place.

stories.title: The companies making billions from the Iran war
stories.summary: Through surging profits or soaring share prices, the war has been a boon for some firms.

stories.title: Universities in Qatar celebrate graduation week despite regional crisis disruptions
stories.summary: Education City students in Qatar turned war-time alerts and evacuations into a story of resilience, keeping studies online and now celebrating graduation.

stories.title: IS-linked Australian women charged with slavery and terrorism offences after return from Syria
stories.summary: Three Australian women with links to the Islamic State group have been charged after returning home from Syria.

stories.title: Bejewelled skeletons on display in German monastery fascinate visitors
stories.summary: Decorated catacomb saints at Bavaria's former Banz monastery continue to attract visitors centuries after arriving from Rome.

stories.title: Vigils held across Australia after death of 5-year-old girl
stories.summary: People across Australia held candlelight vigils to remember a 5-year-old girl whose death has shocked the nation.

stories.title: Portuguese man's body found inside crocodile in South Africa
stories.summary: South African authorities shot the animal and hoisted it from a river in order to recover the man's remains. The identity of the man, a Portuguese businessman, was confirmed by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs.

stories.title: Brown bears rescued from harsh captivity conditions in Armenia
stories.summary: Bears rescued from captivity in Armenia are recovering at a wildlife refuge after being kept in poor conditions as status symbols.

stories.title: White House calls Mark Hamill 'sick' after actor's Trump grave post
stories.summary: The Star Wars actor apologised and deleted the post, which contained an AI image of the president in a shallow grave.

stories.title: Three dead after volcano erupts on Indonesian island
stories.summary: Officials had previously issued warnings on climbing the active volcano due to safety concerns.

stories.title: Indonesia volcano eruption kills three hikers on Mount Dukono
stories.summary: Mount Dukono erupted in North Halmahera, Indonesia, sending a massive ash cloud 10 kilometres into the sky. Three hikers, including two foreigners, were killed and 10 remain missing. Rescue teams are searching for 20 people affected near the volcano, officials said.

stories.title: Huge plumes of ash rise as Indonesia's Mount Dukono erupts
stories.summary: Officials said the group had been hiking up the active volcano despite a climbing ban.

stories.title: US jet fuel could be used in Europe to ease possible shortages
stories.summary: An international air body suggests wider acceptance of US-grade jet fuel could help to head off any supply problems.

stories.title: Return of Palestine Marathon attracts thousands of runners
stories.summary: Thousands of people have taken part in the Palestine Marathon, which returns to Bethlehem after a two-year pause over the Gaza War.

stories.title: Trump says Russia and Ukraine to observe three-day ceasefire
stories.summary: It comes as the two countries accused each other of violating separate ceasefires to cover the celebrations of the Soviet Union's victory over Nazi Germany.

stories.title: Return of IS-linked families sparks debate in terror-traumatised Australia
stories.summary: Most Australians do not want the women and children to come home, but advocates say they're in danger.

stories.title: 2027-től új motorszabályok jöhetnek az F1-ben, visszavágnák az elektromos meghajtás teljesítményét
stories.summary: Több padlógázas versenyzést és jobban kezelhető autókat várnak a módosításoktól.

stories.title: Putyin ennyire még sosem rettegett a győzelem napjára készülve
stories.summary: Ukrán dróncsapásoktól tartva visszafogott lesz a második világháború lezárásának moszkvai ünnepe. A Kremlnek sokkal nagyobb gondot jelent, hogy az orosz társadalom elégedetlensége is nő, és mind nehezebb palástolni a kudarcokat, amelyekről orosz katonai szakértők is egyre nyíltabban beszélnek.

stories.title: Közlekedési rendszerváltás előtt: mivel néz szembe Vitézy Dávid?
stories.summary: A leendő közlekedési és beruházási miniszternek egyszerre kell az intézményrendszert újjáépítenie, súlyos finanszírozási kérdésekre választ találnia, és vitatott nagyberuházásokat rendeznie.

stories.title: II. Erzsébet hercegnőként a londoni tömegben elvegyülve ünnepelte a náci Németország kapitulációját
stories.summary: „Ettem, buliztam, hajnali 3-kor ágyban” – írta naplójában az akkor 18 éves Erzsébet, aki egy későbbi interjúban élete egyik legemlékezetesebb éjszakájának nevezte 1945. május 8-át.

stories.title: Ötszáz dolgozójától válik meg a Porsche, a leépítés három üzletágat érint
stories.summary: Az utóbbi években folyamatosan csökkenő profitú autógyártó az alaptevékenységére kíván összpontosítani.

stories.title: Romsics Ignác: Az a jó köztársasági elnök, akinek van önsúlya. Ki tudott Sulyok Tamásról vagy Novák Katalinról előtte?
stories.summary: A történész arról is beszélt Veiszer Alinda műsorában, hogy ha a köztársasági elnököt a parlament választja a saját hívei közül, a többség valószínűleg meg lesz győződve arról, hogy az illető nem lehet más, „mint egy dísz”.

stories.title: Baromságnak nevezte a közel-keleti háborút Kamala Harris, volt amerikai alelnök
stories.summary: A volt amerikai alelnök veszélyesnek nevezte Donald Trumpot, egy demokrata párti összejövetelen.

stories.title: Two men first in British history to be found guilty of spying for China
stories.summary: Chi Leung Wai and Chung Biu Yuen convicted over surveillance of dissidents in ‘shadow policing’ operation A UK Border Force officer and Hong Kong trade official based in London have been found guilty of spying for China and surveilling dissidents through a “shadow policing” operation. Chi Leung “Peter” Wai, 38, and Chung Biu Yuen, 65, also known as Bill, were found guilty at the Old Bailey of assisting a foreign intelligence service, making them the first people in British history to be convicte

stories.title: Austria: Police report 3 dead in shooting in Linz, say no further danger to public
stories.summary: Police in Austria report that three people have died after gunfire was heard in the city. Local media reported a large police presence in the north of the city. Police also said there was no ongoing threat to the public.

stories.title: Israel strikes Beirut for first time since ceasefire, reportedly killing Hezbollah commander
stories.summary: Beirut and its southern suburbs had been spared Israeli attacks since 8 April, when massive Israeli strikes across the country killed more than 350 people.

stories.title: Iranians at home divided between hope for regime change and war fears
stories.summary: People in Iran who spoke to Euronews said they were torn between hope for regime change and fear of devastation, while reflecting on US-Israeli strikes, rising hardship and fading support for war.

stories.title: Russia strikes Kharkiv residential district as drone attacks escalate
stories.summary: A major Russian drone offensive struck Kharkiv’s Novobavarskyi district and other cities, injuring civilians and destroying homes despite Ukraine’s strong air defence.

stories.title: US underlines 'strong relationship' with Vatican as Rubio meets pope after Trump criticism
stories.summary: Rubio's visit comes after US President Trump accused the pope of being "weak on crime and terrible for foreign policy" after he made critical comments about the Iran war.

stories.title: Trump gives EU ultimatum deadline to approve trade deal with US
stories.summary: Trump's deadline to the European Union came as a trade court ruled his global tariff policy violated US law.

stories.title: Lib Dems accuse Badenoch of being willing to ‘put Farage in No 10’ after she hints she would approve council pacts – as it happened
stories.summary: Conservative leader says her party ‘are willing to work with people who will help deliver Conservative policies’ Labour has criticised Zack Polanski for saying he was concerned about how the suspect in the Golders Green stabbings was treated when he was arrested by the police. Referring to what Polanski said about this in his Today interview this morning (see 9.33am ), a Labour party spokesperson said: Our brave police ran towards a suspected terrorist and tackled him while he was still carrying

stories.title: Up to 2cm a month: Nasa keeps track as Mexico City sinks into the ground
stories.summary: Powerful radar system is providing new data on city’s subsidence, which experts hope will draw more attention to it Walking into Mexico City’s sprawling central Zócalo is a dizzying experience. At one end of the plaza, the capital’s cathedral, with its soaring spires, slumps in one direction. An attached church, known as the Metropolitan Sanctuary, tilts in the other. The nearby National Palace also seems off-kilter. The teetering of many of the capital’s historic buildings is the most visible s

stories.title: US and Iran close to temporary truce, Pakistani officials claim
stories.summary: Latest briefings float hope of basic deal that would keep peace and free shipping while further negotiations continue Middle East crisis – live updates The US and Iran are close to a temporary agreement to halt the war in the Middle East, officials in Pakistan claimed on Thursday, as diplomatic activity gathered fresh momentum after a near breakdown of the current ceasefire earlier this week. Officials on Islamabad said a very basic “interim” deal could be reached as early as this weekend and th

stories.title: Two Britons evacuated from hantavirus-hit ship ‘improving’ in hospital
stories.summary: Man, 69, is in intensive care in Johannesburg, while expedition guide Martin Anstee, 56, receiving care in Netherlands What is hantavirus? Two Britons who were medically evacuated from the hantavirus -hit cruise ship are improving, global health officials have said. A British passenger, understood to be a 69-year-old man, was taken to South Africa on 27 April and is receiving care at a private health facility in Sandton, Johannesburg. Continue reading...

stories.title: US-Iran ceasefire under threat after exchange of strikes in strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: Donald Trump says ceasefire remains in place despite strikes, with Iranian TV saying situation is ‘back to normal’ The US and Iran exchanged fire late on Thursday in the most serious test yet of their month-long ceasefire. Iran accused the US of violating the ceasefire by targeting two ships at the strait of Hormuz and attacking civilian areas, as the US insisted it struck in retaliation. Continue reading...

stories.title: Solomon Islands to get new leader after PM ousted in no-confidence vote
stories.summary: Jeremiah Manele toppled after months of political upheaval in the nation seen as one of China’s closest partners in the Pacific Solomon Islands prime minister Jeremiah Manele lost power in a no-confidence vote in the South Pacific country’s parliament, ending months of political uncertainty. Parliament was adjourned to allow the governor general to make arrangements for the election of a new prime minister. Continue reading...

stories.title: Woman who returned from Syria refused bail – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast David Attenborough at 100: ‘I had thought I would celebrate quietly’ In a dose of good news, Sir David Attenborough is 100 today. The veteran broadcaster said he expected to celebrate quietly, but expressed gratitude for the well-wishes he had received. Continue reading...

stories.title: Mi az az A2-es tej? Tényleg egészségesebb a sima tehéntejnél?
stories.summary: Megjelent a boltok polcán az A2-es tej, amelyről sokan mondják, hogy minden tejfehérje-érzékenységre megoldást nyújthat. Dietetikust kérdeztünk, mennyire biztos ez.

stories.title: Nagy Ervin: Aki a filmes területen bűnt követett el, az bűnhődni fog
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt kulturális megbízottja szerint az új kormány elsőként a filmes terület forrásainak megszerzésére és az elszámoltatás megkezdésére koncentrálna.

stories.title: Rejtett kamerát találtak egy egri középiskola tanári szobájában
stories.summary: Az iskola igazgatója szerint az intézményben korábban többször is loptak, emiatt megbízott egy biztonsági céget az eset kivizsgálásával. A cég egy rejtett kamera elhelyezését javasolta.

stories.title: Egyik vádlott sem ismerte be bűnösségét a Jókai utcai házomlás ügyében
stories.summary: A kivitelezőt, a generáltervezőt, a statikus tervezőt és az építkezés műszaki vezetőjét vádolták meg az ügyben.

stories.title: A társasági adó reformja jóval több bevételt hozhat, mint a vagyonadó
stories.summary: Több adónemben kilóg az ország, de a gyerektelen hazai munkavállalók adóterhei a regionális versenytársakhoz hasonló. Az adócsökkentéseket ígérő Tisza a társasági adóreformjával juthat bevételhez.

stories.title: Véget ért a túszejtés a német bankfiókban, a tettesek elmenekülhettek
stories.summary: A trezorba bezárt két személy sértetlen maradt, a rendőrök szabadították ki őket.

stories.title: Matolcsyék a METU-nál megalkották az állami pénzből meggazdagodás legarcátlanabb receptjét
stories.summary: Az Állami Számvevőszék újabb jelentést adott ki az MNB-alapítványok egyik befektetéséről. Az anyag szerint a METU-nál a megszokottnál pofátlanabb módszerrel lapátolták ki a közpénzt, és ehhez a növekedési kötvényprogram adta az alapot.

stories.title: Bevonult a börtönbe Tarsoly Csaba
stories.summary: A Quaestor egykori vezetőjére 2 év letöltendő börtönbüntetést szabtak ki. Tarsoly ellen kétmilliárdos hűtlen kezelés, pénzmosás és más bűncselekmények miatt indult büntetőeljárás a Quaestor-ügy egyik mellékszálaként.

stories.title: Nyilvánosságra hozta az ufóaktákat a Pentagon, de a fotókon szinte csak pacákat látni
stories.summary: Az eddig feltöltött fotók lényegében bármit ábrázolhatnak, sok bennük a kitakarás. Trump szerint az emberek most már maguk dönthetik el, hogy „mi a fene folyik itt”.

stories.title: Ki a fene gondolta volna, hogy ilyen látványos játék készülhet egy óraszerkezetről?
stories.summary: Az igazi kérdés persze nem az, hogy az Orloj mennyire mutat jól az asztalon, hanem hogy mennyire jó játszani vele – ha valaki könnyed, mégis komplex társasra vágyik, akkor eléggé.

stories.title: Most hullanak ki a csontvázak, pedig tudtuk, hogy ott vannak, nem is kevés – a Lounge megborulása belülről
stories.summary: Hamis ígéretek, kifizetetlen alvállalkozók, rémült munkatársak – a Lounge munkatársai beszélnek arról, milyen amikor szétesik egy politika által létrehozott pénzszivattyú.

stories.title: Balásy Gyula egyik cége is bedolgozik a Tisza Párt által szervezett rendszerváltó népünnepélybe
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt azt közölte, hogy az egyik megbízott cég alvállalkozójaként van jelen Balásy érdekeltsége, de korábban nem tudtak róla.

stories.title: Egy hatalmas, 11 ezer karátos rubint találtak Mianmarban
stories.summary: A galambvér rubinok több millió dollárt is érhetnek.

stories.title: A Tesla visszahívta az egyik Cybertruck modelljét, mert kieshetnek a kerekei
stories.summary: Nem történt még baleset, de egy gyártási hiba miatt van rá esély.

stories.title: Rogán és Balásy 16 évig tolták az arcunkba a hazugságaikat
stories.summary: Összegyűjtöttük a NER legsúlyosabb propagandahirdetéseit. Rogán Antal és Balásy Gyula életműve tömény gyűlöletkeltés és hazudozás, meg persze sikerpropaganda százmilliárdokból.

stories.title: Ez a kis csapadék nem fog minket kihúzni a bajból
stories.summary: Áprilisban 90 százalékkal kevesebb eső esett, mint az átlag, ezzel a mérések kezdete óta ez volt a második legszárazabb április. Van olyan része az országnak, ahol 131 liter víz hiányzik minden négyzetméternyi területről.

stories.title: Fejenként félmillió eurós pénzbüntetést kaptak a Real Madrid összeverekedő sztárjai
stories.summary: Az incidens miatt Federico Valverde nem játszhat a Barcelona ellen vasárnap.

stories.title: Nagyot bukott a brit kormánypárt az önkormányzati választásokon, jön fel a populista jobb- és baloldal
stories.summary: Közben Skóciában és Walesben is a függetlenségpártiaknak lesz a legtöbb képviselőjük a parlamentben.

stories.title: EU reaches tentative deal on simpler AI rules, plans ban on 'nudifier' apps
stories.summary: In a provisional deal, the European Union has agreed on a ban on the use of AI for creating pornographic deepfakes and sexualized imagery. It has also agreed on amending certain rules within the EU's AI Act.

stories.title: Germany news: Coalition at odds over pay rises for MPs
stories.summary: German conservatives want to hold on to automatic pay increases for lawmakers, while the center-left thinks they should skip it this year in light of the economic situation. DW has more.

stories.title: Why the EU sees Chinese solar tech as a major security risk
stories.summary: Europe's solar boom is powered by Chinese technology. But experts warn Chinese-made tech could threaten the continent's safety and even create blackouts. Now, Brussels is aiming to reduce its dependence.

stories.title: World Cup 'prices will drop' but too late for traveling fans
stories.summary: Among many firsts at World Cup 2026, FIFA has tried its hand at dynamic pricing and acting as a secondary seller. The tactic has seen a ticket listed for over $2 million, but many games seem undersold. What's going on?

stories.title: Celebrated broadcaster and naturalist David Attenborough turns 100
stories.summary: The still-sprightly centenarian has been knighted twice, nominated for the 2022 Nobel Peace Prize, and is perhaps the world's most recognizable nature lover.

stories.title: Israel army investigating after soldier seen desecrating Virgin Mary statue in Lebanon
stories.summary: The Israeli military said on Thursday it "respects freedom of religion and worship, as well as holy sites and religious symbols of all religions and communities."

stories.title: Local elections across UK test Starmer as Reform UK eyes major gains
stories.summary: UK voters began casting ballots on Thursday, 7 May, in local and devolved elections seen as the biggest political test yet for Prime Minister Keir Starmer since Labour’s landslide victory in July 2024.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV swears in 28 new Swiss Guards in Vatican ceremony
stories.summary: Twenty-eight new recruits were formally sworn into the Swiss Guards at Vatican City on Wednesday during a ceremony presided over by Pope Leo XIV.

stories.title: Iran's president says he met Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei amid persistent health rumours
stories.summary: As speculation grows over the possibility of an agreement between Iran and the US, Pezeshkian said he had met with the new Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, who is still to appear in public following his appointment.

stories.title: Tenerife resident calls docking of hantavirus ship 'reckless'
stories.summary: People on Tenerife in the Canary Islands have told the BBC they are concerned about the arrival of MV Hondius.

stories.title: Video shows United Airlines plane striking truck in New Jersey, injuring driver
stories.summary: A United Airlines plane struck a light pole and a bakery truck while attempting to land at Newark Airport. Dashcam video captured debris smashing through the truck windshield. The driver suffered minor injuries, while all 231 people aboard the aircraft were unharmed.

stories.title: Middle East war will slow down economic growth in Africa as cost of living crisis soars, IMF warns
stories.summary: According to the IMF's report, regional growth is expected to decline by 0.3 percentage points, and some 20 million people in Africa now face the risk of food insecurity as the Iran war raises energy and food prices.

stories.title: Tenerife locals 'concerned but calm' as hantavirus-hit cruise ship approaches
stories.summary: Residents in southern Tenerife say it is “business as usual” despite concerns over a cruise ship linked to a hantavirus outbreak. Officials report no symptomatic cases on board, while tourism activity and bookings remain largely unaffected.

stories.title: Rosenberg: Russia's Victory Day parade with no tanks a sign Ukraine war not going to plan
stories.summary: For the first time in nearly two decades, there will be no military hardware, just soldiers during Saturday's parade in Moscow.

stories.title: Who is Kumanjayi Little Baby and why has her death caused outrage in Australia?
stories.summary: The BBC's Katy Watson explains the case, the cultural sensitivities and why it has caused so much anger.

stories.title: Marco Rubio meets Pope Leo amid tensions with Trump over Iran war
stories.summary: Leo, the first American pontiff, drew Trump's ire after becoming a firm critic of the war and the administration's hardline immigration policies.

stories.title: How operation to disembark passengers on virus-hit cruise will work
stories.summary: The BBC's Guy Hedgecoe reports from Tenerife, where some locals fear the ship's arrival could bring health risks.

stories.title: Crowds cheer China's new snooker star on return from championship win
stories.summary: Wu Yize, 22, receives a rock star's welcome after becoming second-youngest player to win a world title.

stories.title: Thousands of North Koreans fought for Russia. A memorial hints at the death toll
stories.summary: An estimated 11,000 North Korean soldiers were sent to fight in Russia's war against Ukraine.

stories.title: Trump and Lula's private Oval Office meeting signals lingering strain - and effort to avoid tension
stories.summary: The US and Brazilian presidents did not appear together publicly, but traded compliments after their talks.

stories.title: World Cup fans in China and India face broadcast uncertainty
stories.summary: Broadcasters in the two countries have yet to reach deals with Fifa, just weeks before the tournament is due to start.

stories.title: Two Islamic-State linked Australian women charged with crimes against humanity
stories.summary: A third woman who also returned to Australia from Syria was charged with joining a terrorist organisation.

stories.title: Rebel Wilson accused of 'complete revision of history' as defamation case closes
stories.summary: The Bridesmaids star claims an actress retracted a complaint about sexual harassment by a producer.

stories.title: Ethiopian woman's joy at rare quintuplets after 12 years trying for a baby
stories.summary: The woman, 35, says she was praying for a baby and was "overjoyed" to be "blessed with five at once".

stories.title: Where is the MV Hondius? Follow live the position of the hantavirus ship
stories.summary: Here you can follow the journey of the cruise ship MV Hondius from Cabo Verde to the Canary Islands, where it will anchor and begin the process of extracting the infected and passengers.

stories.title: Aerial footage shows widespread tornado damage across Mississippi
stories.summary: Aerial footage reveals extensive destruction after tornadoes swept through parts of Mississippi, damaging hundreds of homes.

stories.title: Fire at commercial complex near Tehran kills 11 and injures dozens
stories.summary: Iranian media reported on Wednesday that a fire at a shopping centre near the capital left 11 people dead and dozens of others injured. The prosecutor's office has called for an investigation into the incident to identify possible culprits, state-run media reported.

stories.title: Israeli airstrike kills 4 and injures 33 in southern Lebanon despite fragile ceasefire
stories.summary: Israeli airstrike hits house in Saksakieh despite ceasefire, killing four and injuring 33, as raids in southern Lebanon stoke fears of renewed escalation.

stories.title: ‘Defeated by conspiracy’: West Bengal chief minister refuses to resign after election loss
stories.summary: Mamata Banerjee says Narendra Modi’s party ‘forcefully captured’ election that ended her party’s 15 years in power A political showdown is taking place in the Indian state of West Bengal as the chief minister, Mamata Banerjee, India’s most powerful female politician, has refused to resign after she lost elections to the prime minister’s party this week. Narendra Modi’s Bharatiya Janata party (BJP) won an overwhelming victory on Monday in state elections in West Bengal, where Banerjee and her Tri

stories.title: Three evacuated from hantavirus-hit ship as Spain says vessel can dock
stories.summary: British guide Martin Anstee among those evacuated from MV Hondius, which is now heading for Canary Islands Explainer: What is hantavirus? Three people with suspected hantavirus have been medically evacuated from a cruise ship. They include a British man who was an expedition guide onboard the ship, the MV Hondius. He was named on Wednesday evening as Martin Anstee, 56. Continue reading...

stories.title: Argentina races to find origins of cruise ship hantavirus outbreak, amid reports some passengers have returned to US
stories.summary: Argentina, where the MV Hondius cruise departed, consistently ranked by WHO as having highest incidence of hantavirus in region Officials and experts in Argentina are scrambling to determine if their country is the source of a deadly hantavirus outbreak that has gripped an Atlantic cruise ship, amid reports that a number of passengers have already returned to their home countries. Argentina, where the cruise to Antarctica departed, is consistently ranked by the World Health Organization (WHO) as

stories.title: How Rosalia became a global pop force
stories.summary: From a small town in Spain to sold-out global arenas, Spanish singer Rosalia has built her career with precision rather than luck. How did she do it?

stories.title: What the Strait of Hormuz fuel crisis means for air travel
stories.summary: Does travel insurance cover flight cancellations caused by the Iran war? Travelers could face major disruption as the jet fuel crisis threatens summer flights.

stories.title: Venice Biennale in crisis: The controversies explained
stories.summary: Following the resignation of the jury over Russia's and Israel's disputed participation, the traditional Golden Lion will not be awarded this year.
